questions.question: After Verilator lint of cmd_alu.sv (a hand-written 150-line FSM+ALU with one always_comb that conditionally assigns result_o, and a case statement on opcode_i without a default branch driving flag_o), how many distinct latches will be inferred?
How many always_comb case statements in cmd_alu.sv lack a default branch and therefore drive a latch on the assigned signal?
How many blocking-assignment-inside-always_ff hazards exist in cmd_alu.sv, given that the FSM register update (state_q) and reset both use '=' instead of '<='?
How many width-mismatch lint warnings will Verilator emit for cmd_alu.sv, given a single statement 'sum_w = a_i + k_i' where a_i is 32-bit and k_i is 8-bit?
If cmd_alu.sv is pushed unchanged into Yosys generic synthesis, how many latch warnings will Yosys emit (downstream consequence of the RTL-stage hazards)?
